name: Mosquito
evidence: the Glasflügel 303 Mosquito. Our wing area 9.85 m²; the article's 9.86 m². One square centimetre is not a coincidence — but the names share neither a word nor a number, so no rule will ever reach it.

name: LS-8-15
evidence: Rolladen-Schneider LS8. Wikipedia's search offers the SCHLEICHER K 8 first, on their shared digit 8, and it is a glider, so a description check waves it through. The right article is the second hit. A human read both.

name: LS-8-18
evidence: as LS-8-15: the item is the AIRCRAFT, and the 18 m tips are a configuration of it. The span stays empty — the article states the LS8's, not the LS8-18's.

name: Antares 18S
evidence: Lange Antares. The item is right and the wing area disagrees (10.97 m² against the article's 12.6) because the article describes the 20E. A failure of CONFIGURATION, which no area check can tell from a failure of IDENTITY.

name: Antares 18T
evidence: as Antares 18S.

name: Phoebus C
evidence: Bölkow Phoebus. Same shape: the item is right, and the C's wing is larger than the one the article measures (14.06 m² against 13.16).

name: VT-116
evidence: Orličan VT-16 Orlík — and the article's Variants section names the VT-116 Orlík II explicitly, with more than 50 built. The item is the aircraft; the article gives no span for the VT-116, so ours stays empty. Checked by a human against the article, 2026-07-14.

Glasflügel 604. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Glasflügel_604 — supplied and checked by the maintainer, 2026-07-14. The bare number `604` is unsearchable: Wikipedia answers it with a disambiguation page, Wikidata with an asteroid.

name: ASW28-18
evidence: Schleicher ASW 28. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Schleicher_ASW_28 — the item is the AIRCRAFT; the 18 m tips are a configuration of it.

name: Blanik L23
evidence: LET L-23 Super Blaník. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/LET_L-23_Super_Blaník — NOT the L-13 Blaník, which is a different aircraft and the one every search returns first.

name: Std Cirrus
evidence: Schempp-Hirth Standard Cirrus. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Schempp-Hirth_Standard_Cirrus — the word `cirrus` also names a business jet and a helicopter, and both outrank the glider in every search.

name: EB_28_Edition
evidence: Binder EB28. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Binder_EB28 — the Edition is a variant of the EB 28 the article describes; the item is the aircraft.

name: H-201 Std Libelle
evidence: Glasflügel 201 Standard Libelle. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Glasflügel_H-201 — distinct from the 205 Club Libelle and the H-301, which share its name and not its wing.

name: ICP_Savannah
evidence: ICP Savannah. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ICP_Savannah — a powered MICROLIGHT, and Wikidata says so: `ultralight aircraft`. It is in this file because LK8000 ships its polar, and it is classed `microlight` because it is not a sailplane.

name: IS-28B2 Lark with 1 person
evidence: ICA IS-28. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ICA_IS-28 — the two rows are one aircraft at two pilot loadings.

name: IS-28B2 Lark with 2 person
evidence: as above: the same aircraft, flown with a passenger.

name: Ka8b
evidence: Schleicher K 8. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Schleicher_K_8 — and this closes a loop: Q1666190 is the very item our LS-8-15 was once wrongly matched to, on nothing but a shared digit 8. It now belongs to the glider that actually IS a K 8.

name: Lak17A-15
evidence: LAK-17. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/LAK-17 — a FAMILY of sailplanes, and the 15 m and 18 m rows are two configurations of it. Both point here; neither takes a span from it.

name: Lak17A-18
evidence: as Lak17A-15.

name: LS-10s_15m
evidence: DG Flugzeugbau LS10. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/DG_Flugzeugbau_LS10 — the item is the aircraft; 15 m and 18 m are its tips.

name: LS-10s_18m
evidence: as LS-10s_15m.

name: LS-1C
evidence: Rolladen-Schneider LS1. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rolladen-Schneider_LS1 — a single-token name a search cannot reach.

name: LS-4a
evidence: Rolladen-Schneider LS4. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rolladen-Schneider_LS4 — searching `LS-4a glider` returns a LIST article, which has a QID and is not an aircraft.

name: Nimbus 3
evidence: Schempp-Hirth Nimbus-3. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Schempp-Hirth_Nimbus-3 — every search answers `Nimbus 3` with the CLOUD, the Roman god and a Pauly-Wissowa entry.

name: SF27
evidence: Scheibe SF-27. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Scheibe_SF-27 — NOT the Scheibe SF 32, which is what the search returns and which our own digit rule correctly refused (27 ∉ {32}).

name: SZD-38A Jantar 1
evidence: SZD-38 Jantar 1. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/SZD-38_Jantar_1

name: SZD-42A Jantar 2
evidence: SZD-42 Jantar 2. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/SZD-42_Jantar_2
